items.title: When an Actor Saves His Career and Life - Colin Farrell
items.author: FilmStack
items.content:
<article>Colin Farrell is now one of Hollywood’s most respected character actors, delivering unforgettable performances in The Penguin, The Banshees of Inisherin, and The Lobster. But two decades ago, he was being pushed as Hollywood’s next leading man with roles in Minority Report, Daredevil, and Phone Booth. His quick rise brought fame and money, but it also worsened his addictions. Soon, his bad-boy image became very public, and he couldn’t even remember filming entire movies. Hollywood began to write him off.

But Farrell’s comeback isn’t just about reviving his career, but also about reinventing himself as a person. From rock bottom to career-defining performances, Farrell’s journey is about growth, redemption, and defying expectations. Today, we’re diving into the rise, fall, and revival of Colin Farrell. We’ll explore his battles with addiction and fame, what finally pushed him to change, and how he transformed from Hollywood’s wildcard to one of its most respected actors. This is the story of an actor who saved his career, and himself. This is Colin Farrell.

Chapters:
00:00 - Intro
01:03 - Rise & Early Struggles
05:25 - His Lowest Point
09:46 - A Comeback?
13:54 - Found His Place

Colin Farrell. In Bruges. The Penguin. The Batman. The Banshees of Inisherin. The Lobster. Angelman Syndrome Son. Minority Report. Steven Spielberg. Daredevil. Bullseye. Phone Booth. Joel Schumacher. Tigerland. Tom Cruise. Hart's War. Britney Spears. The Recruit. Alexander. Biggest Box Office Bomb. Miami Vice. Terrence Malick. The New World. Crazy Heart. The Imaginarium of Doctor Parnassus. Horrible Bosses. Fright Night. Seven Psychopaths. Martin McDonagh. Yorgos Lanthimos. True Detective. Fantastic Beasts. Widows. The Gentlemen. The North Water. After Yang. Thirteen Lives. 

If you like this video don't forget to leave a like, and if you're interested in videos about movies and the film industry in general, make sure to subscribe to FilmStack for more great content.

If you have any other ideas for videos, leave a comment and I might make a video with your idea.

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/realfilmstack/

#colinfarrell    #dedication    #bestactor</article>

items.title: ANOTHER Gremlins 3 Pitch!!!
items.author: RedLetterMedia
items.content:
<article>In Joe Dante's spirit of anarchy and self-aware commentary on sequels that was Gremlins 2, it's only natural to do that again! But bigger!! This time with the ghoulish things that are soulless corporate reboots. 

A real life Gizmo lives in Joe Dante's basement and REAL Gremlins are unleashed on the set of the pathetic "Gremlins” soft reboot. A lazy director-for-hire, a moronic studio producer, and clueless actors suffer the consequences as Zach Galligan intentionally sabotages the film. After realizing the mistake he’s made, Zach and his co-star son Finn Wolfhard must stop the Gremlins before the escape the studio lot, or worse knock down the famed Warner Brothers water tower! 

Some notes for after viewing:

Of course Stripe is hiding inside a Gizmo costume. Makes much more sense.
And Phoebe Cates will return to acting as she'll be the one who plays Snow White in the fake film reboot trap. That's an obvious one we didn't think of. 

Thanks for listening!!!</article>

items.title: Star Trek: Section 31 - re:View
items.author: RedLetterMedia
items.content: <article>The ultimate cosmic confusion has set in for these two Graveyard Grandpas™ as they watch the newest “Star Trek” “Movie” Star Trek: Section 31. A flop years in the making costing Paramount Plus hundreds of millions of dollars putting them nearly 900 billion dollars in debt. And also costing them the good will of zillions of fans (except for really really dumb ones) all for one man, Alex Trebek. So when Star Trek Discovery was first made. Michelle Yeoh plays a character called Phillip George. She was Bo Burnham’s Captain on the USS Shengie. Burnham screwed up. Georgie Plagu died. Then in the mirror verse, Shelly Burnham discovered Phillp's Georgous has a clone called Evil lady Empress. The leader of the evil Terran Umpire. That evil character would stick around and play games with yum yum girl, punching baddies and saying cool lines like, "wanna go make that man scream?" then I'm not sure what happened to her character? I think she few into Kurtzman’s space hole and went to the future. At some point she must have traveled back in time, but not all the way back because she owns a space station shaped like a birth control pill dispenser and is friends with Rachel Garrett from Yesterday's Enterprise. Like, she was her age like maybe 68 years after the Events in Discovery? I know Michelle Yeoh looks youthful, but c’mon! Not 147 years old! Either way, Alex Kurtzmop thought this character was so well like she gets her own show. He plan for it. Then the China virus plague happened and everyone in California had to hide in their homes and wear masks. No filming! Says someone! Then when that cleared up Yeoh wins an Oscar and can no longer be paid peanuts for playing space psychopath. Tell me about it! Show me the money! Put that money everywhere everything is all at once! She told Kurtzmop where he could stick his hot dog fingers. So because of contracts and cruelty Kurtzmop decided to make Yeoh play space witch one more time for Star Trek. What a disaster. Thank god this is just a "movie" and not 10 episodes of this trash. Saved me some time. What a gas! Flat unfunny characters. Lame plot. Meh effects. I've soiled diapers that look more stylish and cool than this crap. This makes Borderlands look like Rebel Moon. Now, these two Graveyard Grandpas™ are a little behind on their Trek cannon. Who is where and why and why? Does it matter to watch this? Not really. Is Phillip Georgo the main character that you're supposed to sympathize with? I think so? Not sure ? I think you're just supposed to think that this wacky group of unlikely heroes are cool for saving the universe from a super bomb. Why do I care about the universe at all? Seems kind of miserable, mean, dark, cruel, evil, cold, unfunny and has lousy visual FX.</article>

items.title: When Comedy Wasn’t Enough – Jim Carrey’s Search for More
items.author: FilmStack
items.content:
<article>Thanks to Displate for sponsoring this video! 
Use our code FILMSTACK to get your Displates discounted or click on our link https://displate.com/@filmstack to get the discount automatically applied at checkout.

With Ace Ventura, The Mask and Dumb and Dumber all releasing within a single year, Jim Carrey was at the top of Hollywood. But for him it wasn't enough. Instead of playing it safer and only doing films like The Cable Guy and Bruce Almighty, Carrey wanted to prove he can do more than make people laugh by giving a go at more dramatic roles. No one would have ever thought that the slapstick king could star in films like The Truman Show, Man on the Moon, and Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind, but that's what he did. 

But the transition into dramatic roles wasn't smooth, and he didn't really fit in a certain place in Hollywood. And as he got lost in his roles, he began to lose himself. So today, we’re exploring this transformative chapter of Jim Carrey’s career. What early struggles drove him to take such bold risks? How did he balance comedy and drama? And did his quest for more fulfill him? This is the story of when comedy wasn’t enough. This is Jim Carrey.

Chapters:
00:00 - Intro
02:34 - King of Comedy
07:27 - Leap Into Drama
12:19 - Uncertainty
16:00 - Legacy

Jim Carrey. Ace Ventura. The Mask. Cameron Diaz. Dumb and Dumber. Riddler. Batman Forever. The Cable Guy. Liar Liar. The Truman Show. Man on the Moon. The Majestic. How The Grinch Stole Christmas. Bruce Almighty. Me Myself and Irene. Bruce Almighty. Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind. The Number 23. Lemony Snicket. Yes Man. Sonic the Hedgehog. Dumb and Dumber To. Mr Popper's Penguins. The Incredible Burt Wonderstone. No Oscar Nominations. Golden Globe Jim Carrey. 

If you like this video don't forget to leave a like, and if you're interested in videos about movies and the film industry in general, make sure to subscribe to FilmStack for more great content.

items.title: Impressions
items.author: RedLetterMedia
items.content: <article>Mike hosts a new game show where Josh, Jay and Rich Evans all are challenged to attempt impressions they have no idea how to do. Mike has randomly selected the impressions for them to do for his personal amusement.  Josh wonders how he got here and attempts to help Rich and Jay as they stumble through the challenges like perhaps children attempting to solve an advanced quantum mathematics problem presented by an MIT professor. It is a hopeless cause, but one worthy of laughing at their attempts right to their stupid faces. Will Josh win? Will Rich win? Will Jay win? Who knows! Also, there's a special bonus at the end of the video.</article>
